name: Apple AirPods
short_description: Wireless in-ear earbuds with on-body audio processing that put Siri and increasingly AI-driven listening features in the ear.
long_description: AirPods are Apple's wireless earbuds, first released in 2016, that pair a familiar earbud form with custom audio chips (W1, then H1 and H2) for hands-free 'Hey Siri', spatial audio, and on-device signal processing. Later AirPods Pro models add Adaptive Audio and Conversation Awareness, which use AI-powered algorithms to blend noise cancellation and transparency and to lower media when you start talking, plus a clinical-grade Hearing Aid feature. They matter for physical AI because they are an always-worn, near-invisible body-worn sensor and actuator that mediates a person's auditory world in real time.

images.caption: Original Apple AirPods with their open charging case.
images.alt_text: A pair of white Apple AirPods wireless earbuds resting beside their open white charging case.

sources.title: Apple reinvents the wireless headphone with AirPods
sources.access_notes: Official announcement of the original AirPods.

sources.title: Apple introduces AirPods 4 and a hearing health experience with AirPods Pro 2
sources.access_notes: Covers Adaptive Audio, Conversation Awareness, and the Hearing Aid feature.

interactions.title: Hold both stems and hear a stranger translated
interactions.description: Press and hold both AirPods at once and the person across the table keeps speaking their language while you hear it rendered into yours, a beat behind, hands-free.
interactions.source_label: Apple Support — Live Translation with AirPods

interactions.title: Music ducks the moment you start talking
interactions.description: Start a sentence to the barista mid-song and Conversation Awareness drops the volume, quiets the room behind you, and lifts the voice in front of you — then brings the music back when you stop.

interactions.title: Run a five-minute hearing test on your couch
interactions.description: AirPods Pro 2 play soft tones at different frequencies and chart your hearing in decibels, then quietly turn into a self-fitting, FDA-authorized hearing aid for mild-to-moderate loss.
interactions.source_label: FDA — first OTC hearing aid software

interactions.title: Nod yes, shake no, say nothing
interactions.description: A call comes in on a crowded train; instead of speaking to Siri you nod to answer or shake your head to decline, with the H2 chip reading the motion of your skull.

interactions.title: Your earbuds count your heart rate mid-workout
interactions.description: On AirPods Pro 3 an in-ear sensor pulses infrared light hundreds of times a second to read blood flow, logging heart rate and calories for a run without a watch.
interactions.source_label: Apple Support — heart rate on AirPods Pro 3

interactions.title: Leave the phone on the table to eavesdrop politely
interactions.description: People prop the iPhone near a soft-spoken relative across a noisy restaurant and use Live Listen to pipe that microphone straight into their ears — an accessibility feature repurposed as a long-range listening trick.

interactions.title: Falling asleep with them in, every night
interactions.description: Many wearers never take them out at bedtime, using a quiet audiobook or noise blocking to drown out a snoring partner or city traffic until the habit becomes hard to sleep without.

interactions.title: A nudge when your resting pulse drifts up
interactions.description: Because the buds already read heart rate in the ear, an idle day of wearing them could flag a quietly elevated resting rate or post-workout recovery trend, surfacing a health signal you never sat down to measure.

interactions.title: Posture and head-tilt coaching during a call
interactions.description: The same motion sensors that detect a nod could notice you slumping or constantly turning away from your desk mic on long calls and offer a gentle in-ear reminder to sit up and face forward.
